task.question: For an agent trying to answer hard web questions, how do Firecrawl, Exa, Parallel, and Tavily differ in context shape, tool-call flow, and retrieval quality?
task.agentGoal: Gather evidence from official docs and benchmark pages, then compare agent-facing search behavior. Multiple calls are expected.

callRationale: Searched Firecrawl official docs for MCP/search behavior, scrapeOptions, response context, and agent-facing usage.
Searched Exa official docs for search, contents/highlights, MCP, and agent workflow evidence.
Searched Parallel official docs/product pages for Search API, MCP flow, objective/query shape, freshness, source controls, and benchmarks.
Searched Tavily official docs for Search API, Extract, MCP, response shape, answer modes, and agent workflow evidence.
Searched benchmark/evaluation pages for hard-agent web search comparisons across Parallel, Exa, Tavily, and Firecrawl.
Ran a Firecrawl-specific quality follow-up because the first Firecrawl search found docs but limited retrieval-quality benchmark evidence.
Fetched the core official docs pages for all four providers to extract context/result shapes and tool-call flow evidence.
Fetched benchmark pages from Parallel, Exa, and exa-labs to capture methodology and accuracy/cost figures.
Ran a Tavily MCP follow-up because the initial Tavily search surfaced integrations and agent skills before the canonical MCP page.
Fetched Tavily MCP and Search API details to fill in MCP tools, search response fields, include_answer/raw_content controls, depth, and domain filters.
strengths: Covered all expected official domains: docs.firecrawl.dev, exa.ai, parallel.ai/docs.parallel.ai, and docs.tavily.com.
Found current benchmark evidence including Parallel April 2026 benchmark methodology and Exa 2026 comparison pages.
Fetched compact official docs for context shapes and MCP/tool flows instead of relying only on search snippets.
Follow-up calls corrected initial gaps for Firecrawl quality evidence and Tavily MCP details.
weaknesses: Firecrawl did not appear in the retrieved hard-QA benchmark tables, so its retrieval-quality comparison is less direct than Exa/Parallel/Tavily.
Several benchmark pages are provider-affiliated, so the answer explicitly caveats methodology and potential self-comparison bias.
The Parallel provider schema did not expose a structured source-policy parameter, so official-source targeting was done through objectives and follow-up selection.
notableFailures: One harness call failed before reaching the provider because the first JSON args path was created under the initial repo instead of /Users/ethanaggor/firecrawl-results; it produced no raw provider response artifact.
Initial Parallel and Tavily searches included some irrelevant or third-party results; follow-up searches/fetches narrowed the final evidence to official docs, product pages, and benchmark pages.
Firecrawl benchmark evidence found was mostly extraction/product-positioning evidence rather than the same hard-question retrieval benchmark used for Exa, Parallel, and Tavily.
